metadata.note: Archetype A2 (anchor == subject), primary_target=null: EUROPE-BALKANS spans 8 countries and there is no SERBIA centroid, so the target gate is unavailable and the bundle must carry all precision. Deliberately ACTOR-anchored, not country-anchored: bare 'Serbia'/'Srbij' matched 833 titles/180d and would turn this into an all-Serbia-news firehose (N1 Serbia alone publishes 389 titles into this centroid). The tight actor set measures 513 titles, 166 of them non-local. Generic domain verbs (protest, election, student, izbor) are OMITTED per spec sec 2 A2: aliases are OR'd with no target gate, and bare verbs pulled in 111 Kosovo/Albania election and protest titles that belong to neither this atomic nor this phenomenon. Per Pillar 2's multi-country-centroid edge case the country name WOULD be legitimate sub-centroid geography here; it is excluded on volume grounds only, and is therefore re-admitted in the low-volume script-distinct languages (ru/zh/ja/ar/hi) and in German, where 'Serbien' cannot collide with English titles. Latin-script country forms are kept out of it/es/fr because flatten_aliases does not filter by detected_language -- a Latin alias in any non-en key matches English titles too, via the weaker substring path. Includes the energy-leverage strand (NIS/Gazprom/refinery licence) by explicit scoping decision rather than as a separate atomic.
